name: BRAIN HERNIATION
definition: shift of normal brain from high to low pressure through rigid structures of skull ← ⇑ contralateral shift of midline structures under falx cerebri herniation of cingulate gyrus across falx cerebri herniation of brain up / down across tentorium cerebelli downward herniation of brain toward posterior fossa herniation of uncus (most medial part of temporal lobe) across tentorium cerebelli displacement of cerebellum through tentorial incisura superiorly = upward (superior herniation of frontal lobe posteriorly across edge of sphenoid ridge herniation of inferior mesial portions of cerebellum (= inferior tonsils) downward through brain protrusion through fracture / surgical site of skull
distribution: deviation of anterior falx with widened CSF space at contralateral side
discriminating_features: shift of ipsilateral cingulate gyrus beneath falx
deviation of anterior falx with widened CSF space at contralateral side

falx: shift of ipsilateral cingulate gyrus beneath falx
deviation of anterior falx with widened CSF space at contralateral side
corpus callosum: depression / elevation of contralateral corpus callosum
ventricle: compression / effacement of ipsilateral ventricle with amputation of ipsilateral
displacement of lateral ventricle to opposite side
obstruction of foramen of Monro → contralateral dilatation of the lateral ventricle +
compression of anterior cerebral artery → infarction of ACA territory
compression of contralateral cerebral peduncle → notching of midbrain (= Kernohan
compression of aqueduct of Sylvius → early dilatation of temporal horn → obstructive
uncus displaced into suprasellar cistern → pressure on midbrain + brainstem
truncation of six-pointed star appearance of suprasellar cistern
narrowing / effacement of ambient + quadrigeminal cistern
"spinning top" appearance of midbrain due to bilateral compression on posterolateral
posterior displacement of sylvian fissure, temporal lobe + horizontal segment of MCA
cerebellar tonsils at level of dens on axial images
cerebellar tonsils ≥ 5 mm below foramen magnum (= line connecting basion with
effacement of 4th ventricle / aqueduct → hydrocephalus of 3rd + lateral ventricles with

posterior falx remains relatively undisplaced due to greater height + rigidity
clinical.Prognosis: good with shift of < 5 mm; poor with shift > 15 mm
clinical.Cx: traumatic aneurysm of ACA / pericallosal artery
